name: Cuencas Mineras – circular from Alcaine through Hoz de la Vieja and Obón
name_original: Cuencas Mineras – circular desde Alcaine por Hoz de la Vieja y Obón
region_intro: Touring cycling around Alcaine, located in Aragon, Spain, offers a diverse landscape characterized by its historic mining basins, known as Cuencas Mineras, and dramatic natural gorges like Hoz de la Vieja. The terrain includes both paved roads and challenging unpaved segments, with significant elevation gains across the region. This area provides a rugged environment for cyclists seeking varied experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Alcaine The most popular touring cycling route is Cuencas Mineras – circular from Alcaine through Hoz de la Vieja and Obón, a 48.9 miles (78.7 km) trail that takes 7 hours 32 minutes to complete. This route explores the historic Cuencas Mineras region and passes through the scenic Hoz de la Vieja gorge. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Muniesa – Stone Gateposts on Dirt Road loop from Muniesa, a difficult 33.8 miles (54.3 km) path. It features unpaved sections and significant elevation changes. Local touring cyclists also love the Estercuel – Obón loop from Estercuel, a 25.2 miles (40.5 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 32 minutes. Touring cycling around Alcaine is defined by its mining basins, gorges, and rugged, unpaved segments. The network offers options for different ability levels, from routes suitable for any fitness level to more challenging rides. The routes in Alcaine are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 2 reviews. More than 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcaine's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Alcaine?
A: There are over 10 touring cycling routes available around Alcaine, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. All routes in the area are currently rated as difficult, indicating a challenging and rewarding ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Alcaine?
A: The terrain around Alcaine is diverse, characterized by its historic mining basins (Cuencas Mineras) and dramatic natural gorges like Hoz de la Vieja. You can expect a mix of paved roads and challenging unpaved segments, often with significant elevation gains, providing a rugged environment for cyclists.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Alcaine?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Alcaine are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952698132/cuencas-mineras-circular-from-alcaine-through-hoz-de-la-vieja-and-obon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuencas Mineras – circular from Alcaine through Hoz de la Vieja and Obón&lt;/a&gt; is a popular loop that explores the historic mining region and passes through the scenic Hoz de la Vieja gorge.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the Alcaine area?
A: All touring cycling routes in Alcaine are rated as difficult, offering significant challenges. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541726" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muniesa – Stone Gateposts on Dirt Road loop from Muniesa&lt;/a&gt;, which features unpaved sections and substantial elevation changes, making it a demanding ride.

Q: Are there specific landmarks or natural features to look out for on the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the routes often pass through historically and geologically interesting landscapes. You can explore the Cuencas Mineras (Mining Basins) and experience dramatic natural scenery in areas like Hoz de la Vieja. Some routes also lead near settlements such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estercuel&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5143126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obón&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Santa María del Olivar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Alcaine?
A: The touring cycling routes in Alcaine are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 2 reviews. Over 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcaine's varied and challenging terrain, appreciating the rugged environment and diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces?
A: Many routes in Alcaine feature a mix of surfaces. The region is known for its rugged environment, and while some sections are paved, you should expect significant unpaved segments, especially on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moyuela watering trough – Huesa del Común loop from Antigua Estación de Muniesa&lt;/a&gt;, which traverses diverse terrain.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on touring cycling routes in Alcaine?
A: Touring cycling routes around Alcaine often feature significant elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952698132/cuencas-mineras-circular-from-alcaine-through-hoz-de-la-vieja-and-obon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuencas Mineras – circular from Alcaine through Hoz de la Vieja and Obón&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of over 1300 meters, indicating a challenging and hilly ride across the region's varied landscape.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by water features or reservoirs?
A: Yes, some routes offer views of water features. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3618839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Foradada Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a nearby attraction that can be incorporated into or viewed from certain touring cycling routes, adding a scenic element to your ride.

Q: What is the average distance of touring cycling routes in Alcaine?
A: The touring cycling routes in Alcaine vary in distance, but many are substantial. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huesa del Común loop from Estación de Plou&lt;/a&gt; covers approximately 57 kilometers, while others can be shorter or longer, catering to different endurance levels within the 'difficult' category.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the historical mining areas?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its 'Cuencas Mineras' (Mining Basins), and several routes are designed to explore this historical landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952698132/cuencas-mineras-circular-from-alcaine-through-hoz-de-la-vieja-and-obon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuencas Mineras – circular from Alcaine through Hoz de la Vieja and Obón&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering insights into the area's mining heritage.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for a shorter, yet still challenging, ride?
A: While all routes are rated difficult, some are shorter in distance. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31848923" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estercuel – Obón loop from Estercuel&lt;/a&gt; is approximately 40.5 kilometers long and can typically be completed in about 3 hours 32 minutes, offering a challenging experience in a more condensed timeframe.
tips: Stretching from Alcaine to Oliete in the Sierra de Arcos, the Cueva Foradada reservoir stores the water of the Martín River, and belongs to the Ebro Hydrographic Confederation. From this point we have magnificent views of the reservoir from the road.

The name of this locality has a very interesting etymological peculiarity. It really originates from the reddish material of the area, known as Rubiello, which in Aragonese resembles the word viello, old. For this reason and because of the sickle that crosses the town, it was renamed from Foz Rubiella to Foz la Viela, which was later castellanized as Hoz de la Vieja.

Obón is a beautiful town, a Spanish municipality in the province of Teruel that is located in the region of the Cuencas Mineras, in the Iberian System and in the valley of the Martín river. It is located to the south of the Cueva Foradada reservoir and highlights the tourist attraction of the Martín River Cultural Park that will not leave us indifferent.
